name: OmopEncounterVisitOccurrenceView
title: Encounter → visit_occurrence (flat)
description: One FHIR Encounter maps to one visit_occurrence row. The visit_occurrence table is the OMOP anchor for linking clinical events to a care context -- every event table (condition_occurrence, measurement, drug_exposure, etc.) carries a visit_occurrence_id FK. Encounter.class maps to visit_concept_id via a small lookup table; period maps to start/end dates; participant maps to provider_id; serviceProvider maps to care_site_id. Planned and cancelled encounters are dropped.

column.name: visit_occurrence_id
column.description: Surrogate key generated from Encounter.id. HL7 IG FML has this as TODO. NACHC uses auto-increment; omoponfhir uses IdMapping; our project hashes Encounter.id.

column.name: person_id
column.description: Resolve Patient reference. omoponfhir throws FHIRException if absent. ETL-German looks up by identifier or logical ID. Our project uses ctx.ids.resolveRef(), defaults to 0 if unresolved.

column.name: visit_concept_id
column.description: See visit type vocabulary mapping. Small lookup table; 3-9 entries depending on implementation. Core consensus: IMP->9201, AMB->9202, EMER->9203.

column.name: visit_start_date
column.description: Date portion (YYYY-MM-DD). Skip encounter if absent (our project, ETL-German). omoponfhir writes epoch 0 if absent. — transform: date(Encounter.period.start)

column.name: visit_start_datetime
column.description: Full ISO datetime. omoponfhir copies the start Date object directly.

column.name: visit_end_date
column.description: If absent, use visit_start_date (our project, NACHC, FhirToCdm). ETL-German uses LocalDate.now() for still-admitted patients. — transform: date(Encounter.period.end) ?? visit_start_date

column.name: visit_end_datetime
column.description: Full ISO datetime; null if absent. ETL-German uses LocalDateTime.now() for CONCEPT_STILL_PATIENT cases.

column.name: provider_id
column.description: First Practitioner-typed participant (our project). omoponfhir uses getParticipantFirstRep(). fhir-to-omop-demo filters for PPRF (primary performer). fhir-x-omop filters for ATND (attending). NACHC defaults to 1.

column.name: care_site_id
column.description: Organization reference. Our project resolves via ctx.ids.resolveRef(). omoponfhir resolves via IdMapping. FhirToCdm creates a Provider from serviceProvider.display. NACHC defaults to 1.

column.name: visit_source_value
column.description: Verbatim FHIR class code (our project, FhirToCdm). omoponfhir stores Encounter.id. NACHC stores encounter ID.

column.name: admitted_from_concept_id
column.description: Requires vocabulary lookup. 0 if unmapped (our project, FhirToCdm). ETL-German stores in post_process_map for deferred resolution. No implementation in the reference set performs a complete vocabulary lookup.

column.name: admitted_from_source_value
column.description: Raw admit source code. Our project extracts via getSourceValue() and truncates to 50 chars. NACHC stores 'Not Available'. — transform: getSourceValue(admitSource).truncate(50)

column.name: discharged_to_concept_id
column.description: Requires vocabulary lookup. 0 if unmapped (our project). fhir-x-omop maps home->8536, snf->8676, rehab->8615, exp->4216643. Most implementations default to 0.

column.name: discharged_to_source_value
column.description: Raw discharge code. Our project extracts via getSourceValue(). fhir-x-omop uses the display. — transform: getSourceValue(dischargeDisposition).truncate(50)

column.name: preceding_visit_occurrence_id
column.description: Previous encounter in a chain. Not widely used for this field; most implementations use partOf to link child encounters via visit_detail instead.
